mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A labor market tightening, indicated by a UNRATE below 5.0%, signals reduced unemployment and higher corporate earnings potential, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE < 5.0%, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns falls below -0.2 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index (INDPRO) reflects stronger manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO shows no significant correlation with SPX returns over 1-year rolling windows, or 5-day forward returns remain negative in 50%+ of high INDPRO growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in industrial production (INDPRO) and payrolls (PAYEMS) signals robust supply-side expansion and labor market strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to explain >15% of SPX return variance in rolling 6-month windows, or returns are negative in 60%+ of combined growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly rebound in GDP (GDPC1) following a contraction signals improved economic momentum, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns remain negative in 50%+ of quarters where GDPC1 shows a rebound, or the predictive power of GDPC1 lags below 0.15 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising FEDFUNDS combined with elevated CPIAUCSL signals inflationary pressures that may lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL explains <10% of SPX return variance in tightening regimes, or returns are positive in 60%+ of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts (HOUST) indicates stronger consumer confidence and demand for durable goods, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where HOUST exceeds 1050.0, or the correlation between HOUST and SPX returns falls below 0.15 in rolling 3-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in consumer spending (RSAFS) signals weak demand, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as corporate earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: SPX returns remain positive in 50%+ of periods with declining RSAFS, or the predictive power of RSAFS falls below 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.
